title: nephelium foveolatum
intro: Nephelium foveolatum, also known as the rambai fruit tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a toxic compound called amygdalin, but can be processed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The seeds can be used to prepare herbal remedies, including extracts, decoctions, and infusions, which are said to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, preparations from the rambai fruit tree are used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
